title: Cache Identity, TTL, Clock Injection, Readonly Copies, and Capacity · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds observable bounded TypeScript resource cache from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of observable bounded TypeScript resource cache while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Create a canonical typed key from endpoint, resolved identity, page parameters, and representation rather than user spelling.
Inject a clock and define fresh, stale, expired, and boundary instants without timer sleeps in unit tests.
Use immutable domain values, controlled cloning, or copy-on-read so consumers cannot rewrite cached or historical data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2801 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile canonical TTL cache decisions, runtime ownership, error policy, and capacity eviction; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Pokedex Ts Cache Eviction Metrics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 1564 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile canonical TTL cache decisions, runtime ownership, error policy, and capacity eviction; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound entries and approximate bytes, implement deterministic eviction policy, and expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, bypass, and load decisions.
steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Cache Eviction Metrics through the concrete observable bounded TypeScript resource cache case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bound entries and approximate bytes, implement deterministic eviction policy, and expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, bypass, and load decisions.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Cache Eviction Metrics precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 1564 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile canonical TTL cache decisions, runtime ownership, error policy, and capacity eviction; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound entries and approximate bytes, implement deterministic eviction policy, and expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, bypass, and load decisions.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: TTL makes a separate capacity limit unnecessary.
